prompt_text: Open source or affordable product analytics tools instead of PostHog
response:
# Open Source & Affordable Product Analytics Alternatives

## Open Source (Self-Hosted)

### **Plausible Analytics**
- Privacy-focused, GDPR-compliant
- Simple, lightweight alternative to Google Analytics
- €20-200/month or self-hosted
- Great for basic traffic/conversion tracking

### **Matomo**
- Open-source Google Analytics replacement
- Full data ownership, GDPR/CCPA compliant
- Free self-hosted or €19+/month cloud
- Feature-rich but simpler than PostHog

### **Open Web Analytics (OWA)**
- Lightweight, open-source
- Completely free
- Good for basic metrics
- Minimal support/development

### **Metabase**
- Business intelligence & data visualization
- Open-source, self-hosted
- Free with optional paid support
- Great for digging into existing data

### **Apache Superset**
- Data visualization platform
- Open-source, lightweight
- Free self-hosted
- Good for dashboards on top of your database

## Affordable Commercial Tools

### **Fathom Analytics**
- Simple, privacy-first
- €14-89/month
- No session recordings but solid events tracking

### **Heap**
- Automatic event capture
- $495+/month (cheaper than PostHog for most use cases)
- Less setup required

### **Mixpanel**
- Free tier available
- Stronger for engagement/retention metrics
- Scales affordably for most startups

### **Amplitude** (Free Tier)
- Generous free option for small teams
- $995+/month for advanced features
- Better UX than Mixpanel for many users

## Budget Decision Matrix

| Need | Best Option |
|------|------------|
| **Pure cost minimization** | Matomo (self-hosted) |
| **Balanced (cheap + usable)** | Plausible or Fathom |
| **Feature-rich free tier** | Amplitude or Mixpanel |
| **Data visualization only** | Metabase or Superset |

**What's your main use case?** (conversion tracking, retention, session recordings, etc.) Happy to recommend more specifically.
